title: Ensure changes to network gateways are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure changes to network gateways are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. Network gateways are required to send and receive traffic to a destination outside of a VPC. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes to network gateways.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to network gateways will help ensure that all ingress/egress traffic traverses the VPC border via a controlled path.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" Internal Only - General o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log-group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = CreateCustomerGateway) || ($.eventName = DeleteCustomerGateway) || ($.eventName = AttachInternetGateway) || ($.eventName = CreateInternetGateway) || ($.eventName = DeleteInternetGateway) || ($.eventName = DetachInternetGateway) }" 4. Note the <network-gw-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <network-gw-changes-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<network-gw-changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id> Internal Only - General 8. Ensure automated monitoring is enabled: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name NetworkGatewayChanges --metric-name GatewayChanges --namespace AWS/EC2 --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for network gateways changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <network-gw-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<network-gw-changes-metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = CreateCustomerGateway) || ($.eventName = DeleteCustomerGateway) || ($.eventName = AttachInternetGateway) || ($.eventName = CreateInternetGateway) || ($.eventName = DeleteInternetGateway) || ($.eventName = DetachInternetGateway) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: Internal Only - General aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <network-gw-changes-alarm> --metric-name <network-gw-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn> 5. Implement logging and alerting mechanisms: aws sns create-topic --name NetworkGatewayChangesAlerts aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol email --notification-endpoint <email-address> aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name NetworkGatewayChangesAlarm --metric-name GatewayChanges --namespace AWS/EC2 --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: Monitoring changes to network gateways helps detect unauthorized modifications that could compromise network security. Implementing automated monitoring and alerts can improve incident response times, but it may require additional configuration and maintenance efforts.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control aligns with AWS API capabilities, as it can be verified through AWS services, including CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS.
- The audit explicitly mentions AWS CLI commands and details how to access and validate the controls.
- The necessity to ensure active multi-region CloudTrail trails and corresponding metric filters, alarms, and SNS notifications can all be automated, as they are related to standard AWS services.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_network_gateway_changes_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` to list all CloudTrail trails and find the multi-region trails.
   - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>` to check if the identified trail is active.
   - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>` to confirm that the trail includes management events.
   - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>` to retrieve existing metric filters and ensure that the specified filter pattern exists.
   - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<network-gw-changes-metric>]'` to list CloudWatch alarms associated with the identified metric.
   - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>` to check for at least one active subscription to the SNS topic.
  
2. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Enumerate CloudTrail trails across all regions to identify the multi-region trait and appropriate log group.
   - Gather CloudWatch alarms relevant to the network gateway monitoring.

3. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - For each multi-region trail ensure:
     - IsLogging is set to TRUE.
     - At least one event selector includes management events with ReadWriteType set to All.
   - For metric filters, confirm the presence of the specified filter pattern.
   - For alarms, check that there is at least one active alarm associated with the filter metric, and it triggers the SNS topic.
   - Verify that the SNS topic has at least one valid subscription.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - The test passes only if:
     - There is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail set up for logging.
     - The trail captures management events.
     - The specified metric filter exists and matches the required criteria for network gateways.
     - At least one CloudWatch alarm is active and configured correctly.
     - The SNS topic has at least one subscriber, confirming alerting capability.

5. **Example Check:** 
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to fetch trail information, followed by relevant filters and checks as specified in the control.

This automated audit approach ensures compliance with control 4.12 of the CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark by effectively leveraging AWS services and their APIs, facilitating a comprehensive and consistent evaluation of the required monitoring configurations for network gateways.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as "Automated" is correct. This control can be effectively audited using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI commands. The use of CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS is appropriate for monitoring changes to network gateways, and these services support automation through AWS SDKs and CLI.
- The approach aligns well with AWS SDK capabilities, and the use of AWS CLI commands is valid for the tasks described.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct, and the CLI commands provided are valid for the described tasks.
- The boto3 method `describe_trails()` is correctly mentioned for fetching trail information.
- The program name `aws_cloudtrail_network_gateway_changes_monitoring` is descriptive but could be more specific to include the aspect of "changes" to network gateways.
- The approach covers essential checks like multi-region trail verification, management event inclusion, metric filter presence, and SNS subscription validation.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

1. **Program Name:**
   - Suggest a more descriptive name: `aws_cloudtrail_network_gateway_change_monitoring`. This name emphasizes the focus on monitoring changes specifically.

2. **Additional Considerations:**
   - Ensure that the metric filter pattern accurately captures events related to network gateway changes. This might require specifying the exact event names or patterns that correspond to network gateway modifications.
   - Consider using AWS Config rules to complement the monitoring setup, as AWS Config can provide additional insights and historical data on configuration changes.

3. **Alternative AWS API Methods:**
   - Consider using `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` for more detailed alarm information, including state and actions.
   - Use `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()` to verify SNS subscriptions, ensuring they are active and correctly configured.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-aligned with AWS capabilities and best practices for automated compliance checks. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach is comprehensive.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the program name to `aws_cloudtrail_network_gateway_change_monitoring` for clarity.
- Ensure the metric filter pattern is specific to network gateway changes.
- Optionally, integrate AWS Config rules for enhanced monitoring and historical data analysis.
